description:
Advances: specled.spec_review

Context: specled_-7md.3's own scoped work (coverage_pivot_touched_first) is complete,
committed, and green on branch specled_-7md.3/subject-detail-pivots (commit 817975e).
Clearing that ticket's validate-stage warning unmasked 3 pre-existing branch-guard
ERRORs that were previously short-circuited (mix spec.check stops at the validate
stage on failure, so the branch stage never ran until now). None of these three are
within specled_-7md.1, .2, or .3's Allowed touches — they are cross-stage / distill-time
gaps. This ticket exists to resolve all three so the epic's mix spec.check reaches
zero findings.

Deliverable:

1. append_only/must_downgraded on specled.spec_review.degraded_leg_state — the
   requirement was classified `SHALL` at base (origin/main) and `SHALL NOT` at head.
   An accepted ADR already exists —
   .spec/decisions/specled.decision.spec_review_change_scoped_master_detail.md
   (change_type: refines, affects: [specled.spec_review]) — whose Consequences
   section explicitly states requirement statements for triage_panel,
   degraded_leg_state, and per_subject_tabs are "refined in place (same ids, same
   normative force, relocated surfaces)... This decision authorizes those wording
   changes." The branch guard does not recognize this as authorization because (a)
   affects: names only the subject, not the specific requirement id, and (b)
   change_type: refines is not in the guard's authorized-downgrade set
   {weakens, narrows-scope, adds-exception}.

   Investigate which is actually true:
   (a) The current wording ("degraded repo state shall not flip the change-scoped
       verdict chip") is a genuine, deliberate narrowing of testable normative force
       versus the base statement — in which case amend the ADR's affects: to
       explicitly include specled.spec_review.degraded_leg_state and set change_type
       to narrows-scope (or weakens/adds-exception, whichever accurately describes
       it), documenting why the narrowing is intentional and accepted.
   (b) The classifier is reacting to a benign new clause that does not actually
       reduce testable normative force (e.g. an added constraint framed as a
       prohibition rather than an obligation) — in which case reword the requirement
       statement to preserve the exact design intent from the ADR while satisfying
       the append-only classifier (e.g. prefer a positive framing over a raw "shall
       not" if semantically equivalent).
   Do not weaken the actual design decision (degraded state must stay
   non-blocking/advisory per the ADR) — this is about correctly satisfying the
   append-only governance contract for whichever framing is accurate, not about
   changing behavior.

2. branch_guard_unmapped_change on lib/specled_ex/review/findings_delta.ex —
   this module was added by specled_-7md.1 but was never added to
   specled.spec_review's `surface:` list in .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md
   (specled_-7md.1's Allowed touches restricted spec.md edits to "verification stub
   flip only", so it could not register the new file). Add
   lib/specled_ex/review/findings_delta.ex to the subject's surface: list.

3. overlap/duplicate_covers on specled.spec_review.findings_delta — the requirement
   is covered by both scenarios findings_delta_classifies and
   findings_delta_missing_base (lines ~276-297), which this project's spec
   convention disallows (scenarios in the same subject must not list the same
   requirement id in their covers: field). Both scenarios demonstrate real, distinct
   behavior (see test/specled_ex/review/findings_delta_test.exs from
   specled_-7md.1) — do not delete either. Resolve by splitting
   specled.spec_review.findings_delta into two requirement ids (one per scenario) or
   by re-scoping one scenario's covers: to a more specific existing/new requirement
   id that accurately names what it demonstrates (e.g. the missing/unparseable-base
   fallback behavior may deserve its own requirement id distinct from the
   differential-classification requirement).

Verification:
- mix spec.check (base=origin/main), run from a worktree branched off the current
  epic HEAD, must report zero branch-guard findings and zero validate findings for
  the full epic diff.
- mix test: full suite green, no regressions.
- If the ADR is amended in place: re-run mix spec.check to confirm the
  append_only/must_downgraded finding is gone.

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex
- lib/specled_ex/review.ex
- lib/specled_ex/review/findings_delta.ex (registration only via the spec file's
  surface: list — do not modify the module's code)
- lib/specled_ex/verifier*.ex
- lib/specled_ex/coverage_triangulation.ex

Allowed touches:
- .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md (full edit authority: surface: list, requirement
  statement wording, scenario covers: fields — NOT limited to stub flips for this
  ticket)
- .spec/decisions/specled.decision.spec_review_change_scoped_master_detail.md
  (amend in place) OR a new superseding/amending decision file under
  .spec/decisions/ if amending in place is not the right call
- .spec/state.json (regenerated)

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not change any rendering/behavior in html.ex or review.ex — this ticket is
  spec-governance repair only
- Do not weaken the degraded-state design decision itself (must stay non-blocking /
  advisory per the ADR's intent) — only correct how that intent is expressed to
  satisfy append-only governance

Merge gate:
- [ ] mix spec.check clean (0 errors, 0 warnings) against origin/main for the full
      epic diff
- [ ] mix test green, no regressions
- [ ] mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean
- [ ] mix format --check-formatted clean
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

This ticket blocks specled_-7md.3 (dependency wired separately). specled_-7md.3's
own commit (817975e) needs no rework — once this ticket lands and the epic branch
advances, specled_-7md.3's stage branch will be rebased onto the new epic HEAD and
re-verified before merge.

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: b4c28666-8ce9-4a61-922a-352ab59212b4
Log: /Users/mz/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/b4c28666-8ce9-4a61-922a-352ab59212b4.jsonl

Commit: 706cdbb
Files touched: .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md, .spec/state.json (regenerated)
Tests added: none — spec-governance repair only; the covering tests
  (findings_delta_test.exs "differential classification" + "non-differential
  fallback" describe blocks) already exist from specled_-7md.1.
Verification stubs flipped: none (findings_delta command stub was already execute: true).

Summary: Cleared all three pre-existing branch-guard defects. (1) The
append_only/must_downgraded on degraded_leg_state was a classifier false
positive — the head clause "degraded repo state shall not flip the verdict
chip" made ModalClass read the whole statement as :shall_not (negative-first
match) where base was :shall, but the clause ADDS a prohibition, it does not
weaken an obligation. Investigation confirmed no actual narrowing, so the ADR
(change_type: refines) is accurate and stays; I reworded the clause to positive
framing ("the change-scoped verdict chip shall remain driven by the change set
alone, independent of degraded repo state") — same non-blocking/advisory intent,
classifies to :shall. (2) Registered lib/specled_ex/review/findings_delta.ex in
the subject's surface: list. (3) Split findings_delta into findings_delta
(differential classification, covered by findings_delta_classifies) and
findings_delta_base_fallback (missing/unparseable-base fallback, covered by
findings_delta_missing_base), with the new id added to the findings_delta_test.exs
command target (command kind needs no in-file marker, so review.ex stays
untouched per Out of Scope).

Verification note: On THIS branch alone, mix spec.check --base origin/main still
short-circuits at the validate stage on the coverage_pivot_touched_first
verification_target_missing_reference warning — that is specled_-7md.3's
deliverable (its html.ex covers marker), and html.ex is in this ticket's Out of
Scope. To verify the branch stage honestly I built the intended integration tree
(this commit 706cdbb + .3's 817975e cherry-picked): mix spec.check --base
origin/main reported status=pass, 0 validate warnings, branch findings=0
(change_type flipped outside_current_coverage -> single_subject), exit 0, and
mix test was 920 tests / 0 failures. On my branch standalone: mix test 918/0,
compile --warnings-as-errors clean, format --check-formatted clean,
deps.unlock --check-unused clean. Once .4 lands and .3 rebases onto the new epic
HEAD, the full-epic spec.check is clean.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: b4c28666-8ce9-4a61-922a-352ab59212b4 (subagent agent-averify-specled-7md-4-44dd3c01287114a9)
Log: /Users/mz/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/b4c28666-8ce9-4a61-922a-352ab59212b4/subagents/agent-averify-specled-7md-4-44dd3c01287114a9.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched (this ticket's own diff, 8620d0d..HEAD): 2 — .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md, .spec/state.json. Single commit 706cdbb.
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (findings_delta.ex, html.ex, review.ex module code untouched — only spec-file registration/statements changed)

Semantic checks:
- Item 1 (modal classifier): CONFIRMED CORRECT (minor wording nit only). Classifier is lib/specled_ex/modal_class.ex:28-43 — classify/1 is a fixed-priority cond (must not / shall not / must / shall / ...), negatives win over positives; one "shall not" anywhere flips the WHOLE statement to :shall_not. downgrade?/2 (modal_class.ex:73-82) treats positive->negative cross-polarity as a downgrade. Detector: append_only.ex:139-166 compares base(origin/main) vs head. I ran the REAL ModalClass on the actual statements: origin/main degraded_leg_state = :shall (origin/main still has the old "top-of-page banner" wording, NO verdict-chip clause); pre-.4 head (8620d0d) = :shall_not (epic .2 added "degraded repo state shall not flip the verdict chip"); .4 HEAD = :shall (reworded to positive). downgrade?(:shall,:shall_not)=true => must_downgraded FIRED before .4; downgrade?(:shall,:shall)=false => CLEARED at .4. negative_removed does NOT fire (that detector keys off BASE polarity, and origin/main base is :positive). So it is a genuine false positive exactly as characterized: the added "shall not flip" clause is a NEW prohibition, but the whole-statement classifier reads it as a positive->negative downgrade. The reword to "the change-scoped verdict chip shall remain driven by the change set alone, independent of degraded repo state" reclassifies to :shall and clears it with no negative_removed side-effect, preserving identical testable intent (verdict chip independent of degraded state). Nit: implementer's phrase "reads a statement's dominant modal" is imprecise (it's first-match priority, not frequency-dominant) but "matches negative modals first" is literally accurate; conclusion + fix are correct. Not a gap.
- Item 2 (surface registration): CONFIRMED present. lib/specled_ex/review/findings_delta.ex at spec_review.spec.md:22 (surface: block).
- Item 3 (duplicate-covers split): CONFIRMED real split. Two distinct ids: findings_delta (spec_review.spec.md:79) + findings_delta_base_fallback (:89). Scenario coverage after split: findings_delta_classifies (:283) covers ONLY findings_delta (:293); findings_delta_missing_base (:294) covers ONLY findings_delta_base_fallback (:304) — each requirement covered by exactly one scenario, none by both. overlap/duplicate_covers (overlap.ex:61-79) fires on scenario-level duplicate coverage; its own fix hint is "split the requirement" — exactly done. Command stub (:430-434) covering both ids is irrelevant to the detector (scenarios only). Both statements read as coherent freestanding prose.
- Integration-overlay reproduction (item 4): REPRODUCED. Merged .3 (817975e) into .4 tree with --no-commit --no-ff. state.json had a content conflict that git rerere auto-resolved (implementer had run this exact merge before); spec.md auto-merged clean; 0 conflict markers remained; spec.check regenerates state.json via spec.index so the resolution is moot to the outcome. spec.check --base origin/main: FIRST run status=fail errors=2 warnings=0 — but both errors were verification_command_failed from ONE flaky test verifier_test.exs:1004 ("command timeout SIGKILLs process group"), which neither .3 nor .4 touches; it passes in isolation and the run showed transient "/bin/sh: specled_cmd_XXXX.sh: No such file or directory" temp-script races. RETRY of spec.check => status=pass errors=0 warnings=0 exit 0. mix test --include integration => 3 properties, 920 tests, 0 failures (matches implementer's 920/0). mix compile --warnings-as-errors exit 0; mix format --check-formatted exit 0; mix deps.unlock --check-unused exit 0. Merge aborted; working tree clean, HEAD back at 706cdbb. Conclusion: overlay reaches the claimed zero-findings state; the sole discrepancy is a pre-existing flaky OS-timing test, not a defect.
- .4-standalone stub/test results: findings_delta_test.exs (the command stub whose covers this diff modified) = 16 tests, 0 failures. Supporting: modal_class_test + append_only_test + overlap_test = 2 properties, 63 tests, 0 failures.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. ADR specled.decision.spec_review_change_scoped_master_detail.md (NOT touched by this diff) states "Degraded != warning ... Partial verification is advertised on the Spec health pane and its queue badge, not by flipping the change verdict" and explicitly authorizes degraded_leg_state wording refinements "same ids, same normative force" under change_type: refines. The .4 reword is faithful to and authorized by this ADR; non-blocking/advisory design intact.

Verdict: PASS

Notes (advisory only):
- verifier_test.exs:1004 is flaky under the full parallel integration load (process-group SIGKILL timing + command-verifier temp-script cleanup race). Not introduced by this epic (neither .3 nor .4 touches verifier code). Worth a separate hardening ticket, but it does not block .4 or the .3+.4 integration.
- Implementer's item-1 phrasing "dominant modal" is loose (the classifier is fixed-priority first-match); harmless — the fix and reasoning are correct.
comments.author: verify-agent

comments.text:
AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: b4c28666-8ce9-4a61-922a-352ab59212b4 (subagent agent-aaudit-specled-7md-4-ff13e9bd0f17f1e4)
Log: /Users/mz/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/b4c28666-8ce9-4a61-922a-352ab59212b4/subagents/agent-aaudit-specled-7md-4-ff13e9bd0f17f1e4.jsonl

Reasoning: Independently confirmed the highest-risk claim (item 1) end to end. Read modal_class.ex:34-43 — classify/1 is a fixed-priority `cond` matching "must not"/"shall not" BEFORE "must"/"shall", first-match wins (verifier's "fixed-priority first-match" is accurate; implementer's "dominant modal" is loose but harmless). Read the FULL degraded_leg_state statement at all three refs and classified each by hand: origin/main (spec_review.spec.md:78, "...shall surface a top-of-page banner...", no negatives) => :shall; 8620d0d (:186, "...degraded repo state shall not flip the change-scoped verdict chip...") => :shall_not; .4 HEAD (:194, "...the change-scoped verdict chip shall remain driven by the change set alone, independent of degraded repo state...") => :shall. detect_must_downgraded (append_only.ex:139-166) compares base=origin/main (:shall) to head, and downgrade?/2 (modal_class.ex:73-82) gives downgrade?(:shall,:shall_not)=true (positive->negative) but downgrade?(:shall,:shall)=false — so the finding fired before .4 and clears at .4. negative_removed cannot fire (detect_negative_removed keys off BASE polarity, and origin/main base is :positive). The reword is a genuine false-positive fix: the HEAD positive framing fully entails and preserves the 8620d0d prohibition's testable intent (verdict chip unaffected by degraded state), matching the ADR's authorized "same normative force / refines" language (ADR lines 71-73, 100-103). Leaving the ADR untouched (item 5, git diff 8620d0d..HEAD -- .spec/decisions/ empty) is the CORRECT call — amending it to change_type: narrows-scope/weakens would falsely document a narrowing that did not occur.

Item 3 (split) verified real, not renamed: two distinct coherent statements — findings_delta (:79, differential classification) and findings_delta_base_fallback (:89, missing/unparseable-base fallback). overlap.ex detect_duplicate_covers is scenario-only (scenarios_of), and after the split each requirement is covered by exactly one scenario — findings_delta_classifies (:283) covers only findings_delta (:293), findings_delta_missing_base (:294) covers only findings_delta_base_fallback (:304). The command target (:430-434) and source_file target (:456) covering both ids are irrelevant to the detector. Each statement accurately describes what its single scenario demonstrates. Item 2: findings_delta.ex registered at surface: (:22).

Item 4: I REDID the integration overlay myself rather than relying on the verifier. `git merge --no-commit --no-ff specled_-7md.3/subject-detail-pivots` (817975e) into 706cdbb — rerere auto-resolved state.json, zero leftover conflict markers; spec.index regenerates state.json so the resolution is moot. `mix spec.check --base origin/main` => status=pass, validate errors=0 warnings=0, branch base=origin/main changed_files=7 findings=0, change_type=single_subject. Cleaner than the verifier's run — no flaky verifier_test.exs:1004 on my first try (validate errors=0 means the command targets, incl. findings_delta_test.exs, all passed). Merge aborted; tree clean, HEAD back at 706cdbb.

Scope: diff 8620d0d..HEAD touches ONLY .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md + .spec/state.json; findings_delta.ex module code untouched (empty diff); ADR untouched. All Out-of-Scope globs respected. No gaps found in the verifier's work.
comments.author: audit-agent

comments.text: APPROVED. Merged into specled_-7md/spec-review-redesign. Closing per orchestrator sign-off.

title: Spec-governance repair: resolve 3 pre-existing branch-guard defects blocking epic completion
